title: Celery Stew (Khoresh Karafs)
content:
Ingredients

~14 tbsp Olive Oil (6tbsp for chicken 8 tbsp for celery)
2 Medium-Large Onions diced
Salt
1kg Chicken Thighs cute into 1 inch pieces
2 tsp ground Turmeric
Salt & Black Pepper
2 heads of Celery sliced into half inch wide pieces
2 bunches of Parsley, stems removed, finely chopped
1 bunch mint, stems removed, finely chopped
6 tbs lemon juice
½ tsp saffron steeped in ~1 cup of hot water

Method
Chicken / Main pot

Heat large pot medium-high with olive oil
Add onion and cook, stir occasionally, until golden brown
Sprinkle Salt & turn down to medium
Add 2tsp Salt, ½ Tsp Pepper & the turmeric and chicken
Cook 3-5 min until chicken takes on colour, then turn to low

Celery

Heat wide pan medium-high with olive oil
Add celery & ½ tsp Salt, cook for 10 min stir occasionally until slightly fried and reduced in size a bit
Turn to medium and add parsley, cook for 5 min and stire often
Turn to low and add mint, cook ~1 min stir the whole time
Take off heat and add to chicken

Main Pot

Turn to medium, add saffron, water & lemon juice, & 1 tsp salt
Once a slight bubble occurs, turn to low and simmer for ~30 min or until it all comes together and the celery is soft but not mushy

title: Chicken Tikka Malacca
content:
Ingredients
Chicken Marinade:

2 cup full-fat yogurt
4 teaspoons salt
2.5 tablespoons garam masala
1 tablespoon paprika
3 tablespoon mince garlic
4 inch squeeze of mince ginger- from tube
2 kg chicken thighs, cut into 1” pieces

Sauce:

3 Tbsp vegetable oil
2 large yellow onion, thinly sliced
4 inch squeeze of mince garlic - from tube
2 Tbsp mince garlic
2 tsp paprika
2 tsp) cayenne powder
2 Tbsp garam masala
2 Tbsp unsalted butter
2x 425g can of crushed tomatoes
2 cup heavy cream
salt to taste

Method
Chicken:

In a medium-sized bowl, add yogurt, salt, garam masala, paprika, garlic, and ginger; combine.
Add chicken to your bowl, toss to coat thoroughly, and marinate for 10 minutes - overnight in the fridge.

Sauce:

Optional. Set a 10-inch pan over medium-high heat, and add enough vegetable oil to coat the bottom of the pan.

Once hot and nearly smoking, add all your chicken in batches and sear for 2 - 3 minutes per side until nicely browned on all edges.

Remove chicken, add additional oil if needed, and lower the heat to medium.
Add onion, ginger, and garlic. Season lightly with salt, and saute for 3 - 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Next, add paprika, cayenne, garam masala; saute until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add crushed tomatoes, stir and reduce for 5 minutes.
Optional. Pour into a blender and blend on high speed until as smooth as possible. Place back in the pot,
reduce the heat to low, add all your chicken, and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes or until the chicken is thoroughly cooked and the sauce is reduced.
Stir in heavy cream and reduce for 7 - 8 more minutes or until the sauce is thick and velvety—adjust salt levels if necessary.
Serve immediately with steamed white rice. Optionally garnish with cilantro.

cup or so frozen veggies

just chuck it all in ya know

title: Honey Teriyaki Chicken - Slow Cooker
content:
Ingredients

1360g Chicken Thigh
5 tbsp soy sauce
2 tbsp dark soy sauce
4 tbsp honey
4 tbsp mirin
2 tbsp ginger paste
1 tbsp garlic paste
Cornstarch slurry - 2 tbsp cornstarch & 4 tbsp cold water

Yum Yum Sauce

½ cup mayo
½ cup greek yoghurt
5 tbsp sriracha
salt, pepper, garlic to taste

Method

Dump all and mix in slow cooker
High 4 hours
Dump 2.5 cups of cooked rice into the slow cooker and mix with chicken
Chopped spring onion and sesame seeds to garnish

1 bunch spring onions

Method

Method

title: Pasta al Woglette
content:
Ingredients

2 tsp salt
1 tsp black pepper
2 onion
4 good sized bay leaves
1/4 cup olive oil
2 tbsp mince garlic
1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
3 tbs Italian herbs
500ml chicken stock
2x 810g cans crushed tomato or Passatta
1 Jar tomato paste
1 kg beef mince (or half pork)
1 packet Italian Sausage (with fennel)

Method

Olive oil get hot in stock pot
Chuck in red chili flakes and 30 seconds later onion
Saute onion for a bit and chuck in garlic
When onion soggy, brown mince
When mince brown, chuck the rest of the stuff in (not sosig though)
Get to simmer and turn it right down low
Every 30 min or so stir
3/4 hours of stirring in, chuck in sosig - can brown in pan on high first if you can be assed
4-6 hours total should be good

title: Pizza
content:
Ingredients

5 cups of flour
1 tsp salt
1 tsp yeast
2 tbsp olive oil
2 cup of water

Method

Mix dry ingredients
Add water 1/2 cup at time
knead for 10 min
let overnight to rise

Sauce
2 tbsp olive oil
2 tbps garlic
800g crush tomato
2 tsp salt
1 large Onion
1 tbsp italian seasoning
Medium high stove put in oil till hot
Put in chili flakes, onion, garlic- soften the onion
Chuck in mince, brown mince
Put in crush tomato, stock, paste, herb mix, salt, pepper, bay leaves
Bring to simmer and put on the lowest low
Maintain simmer and stir every 30 min for 4 hours

title: Tiramisu
content:
Ingredients

3 eggs separated
⅓ cup caster sugar
½ tsp vanilla extract
250g mascarpone
2 cup coffee

2 double shots
3x 1/3 cup water

30 lady fingers
Cacao powder

Method

Beat yolks and sugar in stand mixer or electric beater on speed 6 to 7 for 10 minutes or until it changes from yellow to pale yellow (almost white), and is thick (see video for consistency).
Add vanilla and mascarpone, beat until just combined and smooth. Transfer mixture to a bowl, set aside.
Clean bowl and whisk. Add egg whites and beat until it's stiff (3 minutes on speed 7, should be all white foam, see video!)
Add 1/2 the cream mixture into the egg whites. Gently fold through. When most incorporated, add the remaining cream mixture and fold through until just combined - don't bash out all the air in the egg whites! (Note 4)
Mix coffee and liquor together. Quickly dip biscuits in and line the bottom of a 8”/20cm square dish. (See video or step photos above to see how I arrange them).
Spread over half the cream, then top with another layer of coffee dipped biscuits.
Spread with remaining cream.
Cover, refrigerate for at least 4 - 5 hours, preferably overnight
Dust with cocoa powder just before serving - either before you cut or after placing onto serving plates.
